verdict_reasoning: LYN is a Src-family tyrosine kinase, N-terminally myristoylated and palmitoylated, anchored to the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane — canonically intracellular. However, LYN (and other Src-family kinases) have documented ecto-display on tumor cells (notably AML blasts and some solid tumors), where a fraction is presented on the outer leaflet and has been explored as a surface target. Cancer cell-state-induced ecto-kinase display qualifies as contextual / cell_state_induced. Not classical surface receptor (no TM, no GPI). Not tissue_restricted_surface (no germline lineage), not lysosomal_exocytosis, not dual_localization (no baseline secretory cycling), not stable_surface_attachment (no covalent TM tether).
predicted_key_uncertainty: Strength of LYN-specific ecto-surface evidence on tumor cells is modest; family-level ecto-kinase biology is better established than gene-specific LYN surface data.
